result:
I would neutralize the engine of scarcity itself—strike the planet with a field where every living being’s day-one needs (clean water, nourishing food, safe shelter, basic medicine, education, a habitable environment) are met without competition or coercion.  

The change wouldn’t be an endless cornucopia of luxury; it would be a floor—arriving at birth and never removed—so that no mind is stunted by hunger, no childhood lost to war over crumbs, no ingenuity wasted on mere survival.  

The world that grows on that floor will still contain struggle, ambition, failure, heartbreak, and mystery. But the struggle would be over meaning, not subsistence; ambition could aim at discovery instead of defense; failure could be instructive instead of fatal; heartbreak would be personal instead of systemic; and mystery, finally, might get the attention it deserves.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
